title: Callitropsis montana
taxonomy: Callitropsis montana is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the cypress family, Cupressaceae. It belongs to the genus Callitropsis and is native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan. The tree is also known as Taiwan cypress or Taiwan mountain cypress. Its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pinophyta. Callitropsis montana is considered an evergreen tree due to its ability to retain its leaves year-round.

intro: Callitropsis montana, also known as mountain cypress or Monterey cypress, is a coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Callitropsis montana include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to promote respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
